_comment: Charter amendment C-2: golden corpora and their expected answers are FROZEN,
IMMUTABLE, VERSIONED artifacts. Drift checks compare COMMITTED BYTES; they never
compare regenerated output against regenerated output.
Nothing in this directory is ever rewritten in place. A corpus change is a NEW
VERSION, with a reviewed diff, and every predecessor is retained -- so that every
historical receipt (an nprobe sweep, a block-size derivation, a recall number in a
release note) stays interpretable forever. A receipt that points at a corpus which
no longer exists is not a receipt.
Create a new version with scripts/new-golden-corpus.sh. It will refuse to touch an

v1.why: The original corpus. Zipf-skewed behavioural motifs; 104 queries in three classes (topic, cluster-boundary, hybrid). Byte-identical since S0 -- and it is only because it never moved that S1's recall-tail finding, and every receipt derived from it, still means anything.
